sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports shortness of breath, particularly after a three-hour bike ride on the weekend, leading to lightheadedness and a feeling of difficulty breathing. He also reports chest pain, but it eventually went away after about an hour. He denies fever, chest pain, nausea, or abdominal pain. The patient reports feeling lightheaded. He had a cough with his first cold in the past year and a half, which resolved a week later. The patient reports intermittent dizziness for one week, worse when standing. He denies chest pain, dyspnea, or syncope. The patient reports feeling lightheaded.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Xygenation is perfect. Neck is supple. Jugular venous distension is not appreciated. Heart rhythm is regular. No murmur is heard. Lungs have a slight wheeze, expiratory wheeze bilaterally. Trace lower extremity edema is present. Chest x-ray is normal. Pulmonary function tests are normal.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an asthma exacerbation triggered by exercise and allergies. A pulmonary referral is indicated.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe albuterol inhaler, two puffs every four hours as needed for shortness of breath. Order a pulmonary referral. Continue imitrex as needed for migraines. Follow up in two weeks to assess response to treatment.
